titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gil J. Wolman Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11411168.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 5, 2018 at 19:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, writings of others, notebooks, photographs, printed materials, audiovisual materials, and other papers by or relating to Gil J. Wolman. Correspondents include Noel Arnaud, Jean Louis Brau, Henri Chopin, Guy Debord, François Dufrêne, Isidore Isou, Maurice Lemaître, Greil Marcus, Sarenco, and others. Writings include drafts for works including Duhring Duhring , L'Internationale Lettriste , and Wolman Résumé . Writings of others include works by Guy Debord, Isidore Isou, and others. Also includes materials relating to Lettrist films including L'Anti-concept and Trait de bave et d'éternité .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, writings of others, notebooks, photographs, printed materials, audiovisual materials, and other papers by or relating to Gil J. Wolman. Correspondents include Noel Arnaud, Jean Louis Brau, Henri Chopin, Guy Debord, François Dufrêne, Isidore Isou, Maurice Lemaître, Greil Marcus, Sarenco, and others. Writings include drafts for works including Duhring Duhring , L'Internationale Lettriste , and Wolman Résumé . Writings of others include works by Guy Debord, Isidore Isou, and others. Also includes materials relating to Lettrist films including L'Anti-concept and Trait de bave et d'éternité .

bioghist.head: Gil J. Wolman
bioghist.p: Gil J. Wolman (or Joseph Wolman) (1929-1995), was a French artist whose work spanned painting, poetry, and film. He was closely associated with the Lettrist movement, and was involved with the publication of the Internationale Lettriste.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gil J. Wolman Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in the order in which the collection was received.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Charlotte Wolman, Hedy Laure Wolman, and Didier Lecointre-Dominique Drouet on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 13, 60 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gil J. Wolman Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: In-process collection. As of November 2017, this collection is closed to readers temporarily, and will reopen when processing is complete. For more information, please consult the Closed Collections Schedule on the Beinecke Web site, at www.library.yale.edu/beinecke.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incoming correspondence from artists, writers, gallery owners, book sellers, researchers looking for sources/source materials, friends, Discussing exhibition organization, invitation cards to exhibits woth manuscript messages, mail art, internal politics of the lettrist movement (who is in who is out), relation of lettrists with the authorities (Berlé, Berna, authorities for censoring Anticoncept. Rich correspondence debord, Jean-Louis Brau, also with writer Dominique Meens

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Invitation cards without manuscript messsages kept in bulk by Wolman in series 5

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Outgoing correspondence. rare case of exchanges between Wolman and correspondent kept with correspondent (provide an example). Otherwise filed in Wolman GJ Outgoing correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With joint letters from France Amann.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter regarding the creation of the poetry and art magazine Dragée haute (1964), correspondence regarding exhibitions, and holiday cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of letters to Jacques Bens, accompanied by no. 1-5 of the literary serial Nos classiques vivants , each consisting of one printed page.

did.unittitle: Association internationale de la lettre et du signe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes joint letters from Bernard and Denise A. Aubertin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Israeli journalist writing to Wolman about living conditions in Jerusalem and personal matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mail art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several letters concern the planning of the festival Artistes en liberté dans le Perche, in Bellême, France.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See Series V, Box, Folder on Belleme festival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, returned by the prison administration, from Wolman to fellow lettrist "Pierre Berlé dit Joell", discussing Wolman's inspector interrogation and deposition about Berlé.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters, some censored, sent by Berna from jail featuring discussions of the lettrist movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mail art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a long letter circa 1953 explaining his reasons for leaving the Internationale Lettriste, a letter from Algeria dated 1953 April 17, and an undated letter from Long Hai, Vietnam circa the mid-1950s discussing combat, Isidore Isou's ideas as they relate to marxism. Most letters are undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters regarding collecting lettrist and situationist material for the Jean Brown Archive.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters, incoming and outgoing, regarding censorship and screening rights for Wolman's film L'anticoncept .

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See Series IV, boxes ///, for more material about Wolman's film.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Incoming letter was transferred from an original gropuping of documents relating to L'anticoncept , now constituting Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal to screen L'anticoncept .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Announcing a screening of Isidore Isou's Traité de bave et d'éternité .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three invitation cards and one painted mail art postcard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mail art (1980), and a proposal for exchanging artworks accompanied by Corfou's curriculum vitae (1984).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a photocopy of Costa's 1974 article "Arte e spontaneità rivoluzionaria".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains circa 40 letters, most undated, discussing the theories and internal politics of the Lettrist movement, disagreements with Isidore Isou and amongst current and former members, the preparation of the lettrist magazines Potlatch and Internationale lettriste, and an evening of "dérive". Accompanied by two photographs; the autograph texts "Réponse à une enquête du groupe surréaliste belge: Quel sens donnez-vous au mot poésie?", "Circonstances de l'esprit nouveau", and "Internationale lettriste no. 2 Manifeste"; and a autograph list of mailing addresses for Potlatch .

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The archivist did not determine during processing whether the accompanying materials were originally enclosed with specific letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding Decelle's acquisitions of Wolman's artworks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entreprise 7891 , no. 1 (1987), enclosed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists largely of postcards signed by Dufrêne and his family [initials "f.g.f.n."].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photocopies of letters exchanged between Dufrêne, Henri Chopin, and Arrigo Lora-Totino debating statements about lettrist and ultra-lettrist artists made by Lora-Totino in his anthology of sound poetry Futura: poesia sonora (Milano: Cramps Records, 1978).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and cards from several art galeries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains offers by director Patrick Leleu to represent Wolman.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Box Folder Spiess, Jacques

did.unittitle: Galleria Antiope / Centro d'arte contemporanae Antiope

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Transferred from [box folders…" background material to Wolman résumé .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one postcard and telegraph receipts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incoming letter regarding the showing of L'anticoncept organized by the ICA and the recording of the audience's reactions to the film, with loan invoice enclosed.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For materials related to Wolman's film, see Series IV box folder

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: transferred from an original grouping of documents relating to L'anticoncept , now constituting Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Largely regarding lettrist exhibitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains holiday cards.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See box folder for joint letters sent by Jessemin and Jacques Spacagna.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two short undated notes and an empty envelope.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mail art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains autograph letters to Wolman, and typed circular letters regarding an exhibit at Galerie Valerie Schmidt Enclosed in letter postmarked 1962 June 09 are four Ektachrome transparencies of artworks in a gallery.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also circular letters kept by Wolman in his file "…" /lettrist tracts files.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Transferred from an original folder labeled "Lemaître". Other circular letters by Lemaitre were placed in series 2 to respect original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mailings of the "Les mots perdus" literary newsletter (1982).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding Marcus' scholarly research, and attempt to locate early lettrist publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Numerous letters from Meens, a poet and writer whose work has featured in Wolman's Éditions inconnues self-publishing label, discussing cultural happenings and sharing his work in progress. Poems and texts enclosed in the correspondence include typescripts of "De l'interruption du dire" (1986), "Scolastique de l'être" (1988), and "Porc" (1990); and typed drafts of Ornithologie du promeneur (1991) with corrections by Wolman. Also enclosed is a copy of La trajectoire du figurant by Philippe Joseph Meens (undated).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lot of fifteen letters exchanged in March and April 1995 between Meens and Patrick [no last name], forwarded in one envelope to Wolman. The letters discuss extensively Wolman's text Les inhumations .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings by Meens found with his correspondence, but not enclosed in dated envelopes. Includes "Journées d'un survivant", "Les amuseries du troglodyte", typed drafts of Ornithologie du promeneur (volumes 1-2), Extorsion d'oiseaux (Paris: Éditions inconnues, 1986), and Leçon de choses (Paris: Éditions inconnues, undated).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one letter on paper stamped "Internationale Lettriste".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Request to use footage from L'anticoncept in a documentary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed with the letter dated 1987 July 06 is Pillet's essay "André Breton à Venise: Le plaisir d'une enquête", from the edited volume Du surréalisme et du plaisir (Paris: Librarie José Corti, 1987).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains holiday cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a short note to Wolman and Guy Debord.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incoming and outgoing correspondence regarding filming the audience watching L'anticoncept during the Exit Art Film Series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the creation of a folding screen collection to be photographed and published.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains professional correspondence from Spiess, an art dealer representing Wolman, as well as personal postcards signed by Spiess and Danouchka Bleskine. Also includes a dossier faxed to Wolman in 1993 regarding the exhibition Particulatoire, Galerie L'espace brut.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For official correspondence by staff of the Galerie Spiess, see box folder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Largely regarding the organization of an exhibition on Guillaume Apollinaire at the Institute of Contemporary Arts (London, England).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Nicole Brenez regarding the organization of a program on Gil J Wolman at the Cinémathèque française.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal correspondence regarding their travels

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Unreadable or no name

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Orphaned envelopes from identifiable correspondents have been filed alphabetically in the main correspondence folders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: groupings of magazines (incomplete runs) directed by Lemaitre, and by Isou ( La dictature lettriste , Arguments lettristes )

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Corss-reference to Writings series for Wolman's theoretical notes on megapneumes and response to Debord's developing situationist theories.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Brackets for creators, titles from incipit. Tracts lettristes (Isou) file folder label - more than tracts by Isou - circular letters by Lemaitre

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a numbered copy with original artwork, as well as preparatory material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original grouping of flyers, booklets, tracts, and clippings relating to René Magritte, Jane Graverol, Roger van de Wouwer, and Mirabelle Dors.

did.unittitle: "Les jeux de l'amour et du hasard: Fragments d'un roman-film influentiel", autograph script with pasted images
"Jusqu'à l'os", printed text with manuscript corrections

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See Series 4 box …. Folder …. For proofs for publication in Ion , no. 1, 1952.

did.unittitle: Bertini, Gianni. "Conversation avec Giulio Carlo Argan", typescript with manuscript corrections
Brau, Jean-Louis, François Dufrêne, and Gil J Wolman. "Limelight" tract, typescripts
"La méthode Romi" / "Étude de structure", typescripts with corrections
"Le point final d'une ténébreuse affaire", typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by "Annexe I: Sur ma qualité de lettriste, sur mon inconditionalité et ses limites".

did.unittitle: Cahiers pour un dictat artistique. Statement on the arrest of Serge Berna, typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Back of sheet features an autograph note from Jean-Louis Brau. Accompanied by a letter from C.P. Matricon to Jean-Louis Brau (dated 18 November 1950) expressing disagreement with the Cahiers pour un dictat artistique's statement.

did.unittitle: Cathelin, Jean. "Thrène pour M.L., cadavre vivant à conspuer éminement en forme de sonnet" typescript
Cinémathèque française. Delivery sheet for the film Traité de bave et d'éternité
Comité psychogéographique de Londres. "Le communiqué de Begnins"
Debord, Guy. Last six pages of the "Manifeste pour une construction de situations", typescript with corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Continuation of the "Manifeste pour une construction de situations," posthumously published in incomplete form in Oeuvres (Gallimard, 2006). Accompanied by lettrist tracts and poems by Debord and others: Jean-Louis Brau, "La corrida chez le prophète" (undated); Debord, "Scandaleux droit de survivre", and "Prochaine planète" (undated); Internationale Lettriste, ["L'indifférence a fait ce monde"] (1954 May 05); Gilles Ivain, ["L'activité principale...dérive continue"] (1953 October 03); Jean-Michel Mension, "Lock-out" (undated); Jean Rollin, "Vers un baptême lettriste" (circa 1963); Unknown, "36 Rue des Morillons" and "Rythme farouche" (undated).

did.unittitle: "Examen de la pureté", typescript with pasted cartoons
"Isou nous deplaît" / "Lochhmar" tract, typescripts with corrections
"Documents sur la création de l'Internationale lettriste + Potlach + écrits" file

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original grouping of documents including a typed lettrist poem, two drawings, and a photograph.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: HALF OF IL no.3?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated for the 1973 recording at the Atelier de création radiophonique on France Culture, with the voices of Dufrêne, Wolman, and Jacques Spacagna.

did.unittitle: "Texticules pour l'os à moelle" / "Discours de Monsieur Savoskesavo au Congrès du Plan de Polarisation", autograph texts

title.part: "En marge": La revue du refus pour une nouvelle participation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies, one incomplete.

did.unittitle: Front de la jeunesse , issue no. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three copies and a paste-up with manuscript corrections.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: See Debord's correspondence for autograph text….

did.unittitle: Internationale lettriste , typed drafts for no. 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts with manuscript corrections by Guy Debord for texts to be published in issue no. 3.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains issues no.1-29 (1954-1957), and issue no.1 (1959) of the new series. No. 1-28 are issued in the name of the Internationale lettriste group, while no. 29 and no. 1 (new series) are issued by the Internationale situationniste group.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Accompanied by miscellaneous photocopies related to lettrism and blank Photosillon postcards found with Potlatch issues during processing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Finis les pieds plats", "Touchez pas aux lettristes", "Deutschland über alles", and "Ordre de boycott" for the the Festival de la Cité Radieuse.

did.unittitle: "Déclaration sur les procès contre l'Internationale Situationniste en Allemagne Fédérale"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the tracts "Au secours de Van Guglielmi!" by Asger Jorn (1958), "Sur l'exclusion d'Attila Kotànyi" (1963), and an address to the Assemblée générale de l'Association internationale des critiques d'art (1958).

did.unittitle: Internationale situationniste II. Bauhaus situationiste brochure

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcription of speeches given by Jörgen Nash and Jean Sellem at the Centre Pompidou, 1989 February 21.

did.unittitle: [ Ion magazine file] "Divers + planches photos Ion "

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original grouping of documents including notes in Wolman's hand and photographic prints of Béla Bartók.

did.unittitle: Annotations to the exhibit flyer for La lettre et le signe dans la peinture contemporaine
"Assez de plagieurs du lettrisme et de l'art supertemporel" tract, typescript with annotations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder of clippings about Isou collected by Wolman, all but three regarding the critical reception of Traité de bave et d'éternité . Accompanied by a photographic print of Alexandre Serge Stavisky.

did.unittitle: "Contre la confusion dans la poésie phonétique" tract, typescripts
"Contre l'Internationale (sic) lettriste", confusionniste et scissionniste, ….", typescript with corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by the typescript with corrections by Isou "Nouvelles explications sur les scissionnistes réactionnaires de l'"Internationale" (sic)".

did.unittitle: "La dernière infamie de Maurice Lemaître", typescript with corrections
"Lemaître corrompt mes amis", typescript with holograph corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a typed circular letter by Jean Cathelin, dated 1962 August 09, responding to Isou's text.

did.unittitle: Le lettrisme et l'hypergraphie dans la peinture et la sculpture contemporaines , partial proofs with corrections
"Manifeste de l'anti-meca-esthétique", holograph and typed drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten and typed version with annotations by Isou of a manifesto signed by Isou, Wolman, Gabriel Pomerand, and Jacques Spacagna.

did.unittitle: "Proposition de plan d'activités des lettristes pour l'année 1963-64", typescript
Tract against the Ministère de la Justice's laws on erotic literature, autograph draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attributed to Isou.

did.unittitle: Traité de bave et d'éternité , partial holograph and typed scripts
[Isou, Isidore]. Untitled script, manuscript and typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript in the hand of Isidore Isou and typescript with additions by Wolman for an untitled sound or film script.

did.unittitle: De la méthode triolectique dans ses applications en situologie générale pamphlet
Circular letters to members of the lettrist group, typescripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed letters addressed to Jean-Louis Brau, Charles Bridoux, Isidore Isou, Jacques Spacagna, Wolman, and others discussing the publication of their bulletin, exhibitions to which they contributed, disagreements with Isou, and the internal politics of the lettrist movement. Accompanied by three circular letters by Jacques Spacagna dated 1962 September and October discussing the publication of Nouvelle génération , and one circular letter by Spacagna and Roberto Altmann dated 1964 December 07 regarding Ô .

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: From the original grouping labeled "Tracts lettristes (Isou)".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original grouping of material related to lettrism [not solely to Isou], mostly dating from the later period in the 1960s-1970s. Includes flyers for exhibitions, recitals, and publications; internal documents; tracts and declarations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Fini le cinéma français", "Les Amis du Cinéma" flyers signed by members of the lettrist group, and "La Nuit du Cinéma".

did.unittitle: Mouvement international pour un Bauhaus imaginiste. "C'est la lutte finale" tract.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Editor-in-chief Maurice Lemaître.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Found among lettrist publications directed by Maurice Lemaître.

did.unittitle: La poésie et la musique lettristes et aphonistes event program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains first and second supplements to no. 18 and no. 20, and second supplements only to no. 17 and no. 19.

did.unittitle: Le port-folio situationniste Volume 1 , unbound photocopies
Primo congresso mondiale degli artisti liberi, ephemera
La revue de psychokladologie et de psychothéie , no. 2
Straram, Patrick. ["Préface, qu'on comprenne…"], typescript with corrections
Szpiro, Maurice-Alain. "La veste", typescript with corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ten page short story. Vendor inventory identifies Szpiro as a friend of Jean-Louis Brau.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: File found next to lettrist typescripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Features several poems by Jean-Louis Brau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript receipt book documenting the deposit of Ur copies in local bookstores.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three circular letters regarding the publication of no. 1-2 of the new series of Ur signed by Roberto Altmann and Roland Sabatier, as well as two flyers and a printed cover design.

did.unittitle: Unknown. ["Erratum: dans le livre de la connaissance…"], typescript

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: File found next to lettrist typescripts.

did.unittitle: Unknown. ["Que tout signe se cripse aussitôt écrit…"], typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Éditions Inconnues self-publishing (haute vie, De l'anticoncept à l'anticoncept, Donner à dire/appel à témoins, le livre des mots, le passage, sens perdu) … Visual for artists books (drawings, photocopies of clippings, photographs) photocopy art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: intersection of artists' book, art exhibition, writings e.g. Haute Vie (Editions inconnues) and Quelques jours en aout

did.unittitle: "De l'anticoncept à l'anticoncept, suivi de Les Inhumations", holograph draft
Appel à témoins , printed prototype under initial title Donner à dire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three pages from Friedrich Engels's Anti-Dürhing .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On 5"x7" notecards, paginated on the verso.

did.unittitle: Typescript under the alternate title Rencontre fortuite
Preparatory work for portraits and drawings, photocopies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whole process

did.unittitle: "Des phrases déchirées dans le monde du 22 novembre", three holograph notebooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four copies

did.unittitle: Maquette of text frames painted in gouache
Paste-up, typed text glued on photocopied frames

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Paste-up fragments, photocopies, and tests found together in one folder during processing. Subdivided into two folders for preservation reasons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages 1-17 missing. Accompanied by an acknowledgement of receipt from Éditions du Seuil, dated 1977 May 03.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: photocopy art

did.unittitle: Photographic prints reproduced in the book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled drafts relating to L'homme penché or L'homme séparé .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On 4"x6" notecards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by holograph drafts possibly related to L'homme séparé , including a dialogue ["L'homme est étendu sur la plage…"].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text written in gouache in large characters on white pages torn in the middle and glued on black cardboard, paginated on the verso.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reproductions of the maquette on beige cardboard.

did.unittitle: Text on tracing paper and cardboard
Printed text with holograph dedication to Charlotte Wolman
["Limité par le jeu des successions…"], printout of excerpt
Des mots rayés nuls , uncut proof with corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (published twice: bound with Le sens perdu de l'interruption by Éditions Inconnues (Paris: 1991) = can't have been the same text!, bound with Les inhumations by Éditions Allia (Paris: 1995).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts found together in one box. Subdivided in seven folders for preservation during processing.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts found together in one box with a note "14 Sept. 91". Subdivided in eight folders for preservation during processing.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts found together in one folder labeled "Peinture dépeinte journal, février 91". Subdivided in four folders for preservation during processing.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts found together in one box. Subdivided in four folders for preservation during processing.

did.unittitle: ["Apparition connue inconnue…"], holograph and printed excerpt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled notebooks with drafts for various chronological segments of Peinture dépeinte .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peinture dépeinte text for 1991 February 18 to 1991 September 07.

did.unittitle: Excerpt (1994 January-February), printout with pasted oval shapes
Printouts of sections for 1994 October - 1995 May
Les inhumations, ceci n'est pas un livre (Éditions inconnues), printout with holograph corrections
Les inhumations , followed by Peinture dépeinte , printout
Les inhumations, suivi de Peinture dépeinte (Éditions Allia), cover design and erratum
"Mars 1995 suite des Inhumations", printout of unpublished sequel
Le sens perdu de l'interruption , paste-ups and proofs

title.part: Quelques jours en août, affiches restaurées

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Installation at Galerie Weiller in 1977

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Decision to classify as "Writings -- books" Fredrick Acquaviva qualified "At the crossroads of […]the artist's book and the installation" (p.34), typescripts and posters were found in different places in the collection, among exhibit ephemera and lettrist publications and Wolman publications in no organic. Appropriating content (here news headlines), breaking down the texts' sentence and word structure to create something new like Durhing Durhing and Fin de communication = conversation with Wolman's writings in the "book" format.

did.unittitle: Typescripts of French and Italian versions

title.part: Vivre et mourir, précédé par introduction et séparation du mot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and ephemera related to exhibitions, and a typescript of the 1968 radio program "Beyond the Word" by Simon Watson Taylor on sound poetry.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials found together in one folder labeled "Documents originaux". Subdivided in five folders for preservation during processing.

did.unittitle: "Wolman / Art scotch / Séparation / Manifestation" background materials files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings and articles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials found together in one folder labeled "Wolman / Art scotch / Séparation / Manifestation". Subdivided in four folders for preservation during processing.

did.unittitle: Pages selected for the exhibition I am immortal and alive , Museu d'Art Contemporani de Barcelona
"Wolman résumé of the preceding chapters", typescript
"Wolman résumé" typescript covering his career until 1987
Adaptation of Albert Camus' L'étranger for theater, holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File of typescripts originally grouped together, accompanied by the holograph note "Hors Texte de la série Je suis la proie des mots 1987-1988".

did.unittitle: "Il faut dire l'art-non", holograph text and printed maquette

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a typed envelope ["Wolman a découvert quelque chose…"].

did.unittitle: "Journal d'une exposition", printouts in large font

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This text was included in Wolman's anthology De l'anticoncept à l'anticoncept (Paris: Éditions inconnues, 1990).

did.unittitle: "Lettre à Guy Ernest Debord sur les formes propres de l'esthétique[…]", holograph notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook containing five pages with a text by Wolman titled "Lettre à Guy Ernest Debord sur les formes propres de l'esthétique et de la situation, les glissements spécifiques de découvertes concommittantes et de la théorie wolmanienne de l'homme seul", dated 1952 December 10, with annotations in another hand. The notebook also contains two pages of notes regarding Le front de la jeunesse .

did.unittitle: "La nuit n'est pas un endroit pour mourir", holograph film script
"Pourquoi je ne participe pas à l'exposition lettrisme et hypergraphie", holograph draft

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Several texts without titles, some paginated. Explain convention incipit ["…"].

did.unittitle: Early notes on megapneumie and lettrism

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: half found in same box

did.unittitle: ["Ils étaient traités d'hérétiques.."], notes on the lettrist movement
"Improvisations mégapneumes" and theoretical writings on art intégral
["La megapneumie prend possession de chacun des signes des lettres…"] theoretical note

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts found together in one unlabeled folder. Subdivided in two folders for preservation during processing.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts found together in one unlabeled folder. Subdivided in two folders for preservation during processing.

did.unittitle: Notebook with notes on theater and phonetics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File of holograph poems originally grouped together that includes several fragments with missing text.

did.unittitle: ["L'art est un jeu d'émigrant …"], poem typescript
["Ce n'est pas tant de le dire…"], holograph note
["C'est le blanc qui limite…"]/["La couleur c'est l'espace incontrolé…"], typescripts
["Cette lumière qui se soulève…"], holograph fragment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One page with holograph text. Rest of notebook is empty.

did.unittitle: ["-Intellectuel! Je décrocherai ma droite…"], holograph fragment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The text was published posthumously in Défense de mourir , edited by Gérard Berréby (Paris: Éditions Allia, 2001).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The text was published posthumously in Défense de mourir , edited by Gérard Berréby (Paris: Éditions Allia, 2001), under the devised title "Joseph me voilà".

did.unittitle: ["Trouver un mot passe encore…"], holograph text on cards
Notes on François Dufrêne and Wolman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an excerpt transcribed in Wolman's hand of a 1975 interview with Dufrêne on Poésie interrompue, France Culture, in which Dufrêne discusses Wolman's mégapneumes; and three pages of notes "À propos de Dufrêne (son appartenance de plusieurs mois au groupe isouien".

did.unittitle: Notebooks with holograph drafts, possibly relating to Les entrepôts d'Ivry
Untitled manuscripts file [possibly a film sequence plan]

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes holograph text ["On nous vante la civilisation…"].

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

did.unittitle: Untitled manuscripts file, including a film script

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Undated holograph draft, and typescript with corrections dated 1953 May 31, for an untitled film script with five parts. First line: "Cette fille est nue, sa peau noire". The script was published posthumously in Défense de mourir , edited by Gérard Berréby (Paris: Éditions Allia, 2001), under the devised title "Joseph me voilà". The file also includes later holograph drafts ["Il faut dire quelque chose.."], ["À la fin des années 40 quand j'étais lettriste…"], and "Sans suite"; and typescripts "De la dil à la dil", "À ma rive étallée", and "Schema de mes formes tuées".

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information, and subdivided into four folders during processing for preservation reasons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the typescript "Constat d'échec du 20 septembre 1978", an holograph excerpt from "La séparation du mot", and a note "Le lettrisme n'est pas le lettrisme".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph texts and fragments, mostly relating to Wolman's concept of séparation. Includes "La séparation des corps" (dated 1981 June 12), "Mouvement séparatiste" (undated), "Lettre ouverte" (circa 1988), and "Projection ou le concept retrouvé, un film de GW" (dated 1988 May 31).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes holograph and typed text ["La création est un jeu d'enfants…"] and ["Plus j'approche de moi plus j'éloigne l'autre"].

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescript ["Le moi l'émotion le mot…"].

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes holograph texts and fragments, and the short typescripts ["Il découpe ses tableaux selon l'art des pointillés…"], "Projection ou le concept retrouvé", ["Il faut inventer la nature…"], "Je garde mon jeu", and ["Viv es un évid…"]/

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains several short holograph texts, including "Le conte à rebours", ["De l'arbre / merveilleuse tenue…"], ["Il y avait un grand front populaire…"], ["Le simple mot de géométrie…"], and ["-Il fait nuit…"].

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains holograph and typed pages, including "Wolman par Wolman", "Wolman par les autres", "À propos d'une exposition qui n'aura pas lieu", and "Un an après".

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the holograph texts "Quelques minutes de la vie d'un homme", "Histoire de Monsieur Pipe", and ["Les hommes sont ce qu'ils sont..."], and the typescripts ["Silence…"] and ["Il y a loin…"]. Also includes holograph fragments related to Wolman's concept of séparation, and others possibly related to vivre et mourir, l'homme separe, les inhumations

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information, and subdivided into two folders during processing for preservation reasons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescripts of poems and holograph fragments.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items transferred from an original folder bearing no identifying information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: collected by Charlotte Wolman after his death. Wikipedia: "The film was shown for the first time on 11 February 1952 at the 'Avant-Garde 52' cinema club. The film was banned by the French censors on 2 April 1952." script must have been written 1951 or earlier

did.unittitle: Censorship notice from the Commission de contrôle des films cinématographiques

Essay "Le cinematochrone nouvelle amplitude", typescript with corrections
Essay "Le mouvement et le rapport de l'artiste, de son film aux spectateurs
Essay "Pour un art de l'anticoncept", holograph
Film script, typescript with holograph corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First page missing .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two complete typed copies with annotations, fragments of other script copies, and of other texts by Wolman.

scopecontent.p: Also contains proofs for Serge Berna, "Jusqu'à l'os", published in the same issue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35mm. Accompanied by a photographic print.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two page typescript with corrections of interview with Wolman about the film, in preparation for its presentation at the Centre Pompidou in 1989.

Petition again censorship signed by J.A. Ramos and E.A. Carvallo

did.unittitle: Tract "Pourquoi les spectateurs hurleront pendant la projection de L'Anticoncept "
Tract "La technique ne décide pas de tout"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Largely for exhibition preparation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For Cannes (1952), and Trente ans de cinéma expérimental en France, 1950-1980 [1982]

did.unittitle: Constitution for the Société anonyme de beaux arts (SABA)
Maison de la Culture du Havre, Galerie de prêt loan receipts
Salon Comparaisons forms and exhibitor card
Maison de la Culture de Grenoble, Galerie de prêt loan receipts
Salon des réalités nouvelles deposit certificate
École supérieure des sciences économiques et commerciales exhibition
Foire internationale d'art contemporain installation preparation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains project descriptions, forms, and calls for public participation in creating Wolman's F.I.A.C. installations. Includes typescripts for "Projet pour le PIAC (Parking International d'Art Contemporain)" (1977) and "Dispersion de la collection Wolman" [circa 1982]; a press release and delivery forms for the installation of fax machines at the L'Officiel des Galeries stand for the installation "Je vous envoie un dessin" (1984); a call for photograph submissions for "L'autre moitié" (1986); and holograph price list for several artists (1987). Accompanied by the tract "Intervention no. 2 L'oreille de Van Gogh" (1974) by Henri J. Enu for the 1975 F.I.A.C.

did.unittitle: Ville de Bellême, Artistes en liberté dans le Perche participant dossier

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by clippings documenting the event.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contract, projet proposal, clipping, and letter from Jacques Spiess regarding the organization of the New York exhibition Color, based on the French exhibition Nouvelle pigmentation.

did.unittitle: Fonds d'incitation à la création application checklist
Centre Georges Pompidou, Hors limites exhibition preparation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loan forms, insurance forms, photocopy of Wolman's "J'écris propre" collage loaned to the Centre Pompidou, and correspondence related to the exhibition.

did.unittitle: Centre Georges Pompidou, Voir de mémoire exhibition preparation
Musée de Marseille exhibition space plan

Includes duplicates of invitation cards and press releases found in other folders of this series, as well as a brochure for a sound poetry event held at the Musée d'art moderne de la ville de Paris on 1967 June 08.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Limited sample/amount representing concepts and media explored by Wolman's art from the 1960s to the 1980s, including art scotch, photocopy art, and séparation.

did.unittitle: Drawings of stylized figures, ink on tracing paper

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 53 pages of drawings in style similar to Duhring Duhring.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Includes mail art, flyers, photographs, envelopes, and paper fragments that may have been kept as source material for collages or photocopy art.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Two copies, caption: "Freud de Gauvin, séparation de Wolman, autobuste de Clavel".

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Three untitled groupings, composed of overlapping press clippings, of a woman's portrait, and manuscript notes.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Signed and numbered 36/40, 28 x 21 cm. [Published in the serial Apeïros , edited by Roberto Altmann.]

did.unittitle: Altagor. Watercolor with stamped address and mottos
Bodson, Guy. Photocopy art "Faites vous même un Wolman"
[Gilardi, Piero?]. Black and white abstract prints

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Series of four drawings printed on recycled paper, signed in the back "Gilard[i]/[y] 123".

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Includes a paste-up for a flyer advertising the exhibition Saintaises (Galerie Raph, 1979), and sketches for a flyer advertising the exhibition Oriances (Galerie Raph, 1981).

did.unittitle: [Koskas, George?]. Untitled gouache painting on paper

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Unsigned painting found next to notes from George Koskas to Wolman.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Piece no. 284 of puzzle 01, and piece no. 23 of puzzle 03.

did.unittitle: Wolman, Charlotte. Ink and pencil drawings
Unknown, "Kluft" geometric drawings in ink on paper
Dufrêne, François. Cantate des mots camés (Éditions Achèle)
Booklet of three sound poetry records (Éditions Achèle)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Likely copies of the sound poetry records by Wolman, Dufrêne, and Brau released together by Éditions Achèle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VIII, Writings about Wolman, contains essays, articles, theses, and dissertations analyzing Wolman's literary and visual art work. Typescripts of essays submitted to Charlotte Wolman in preparation for the retrospective volume Défense de mourir (Paris: Éditions Allia, 2001) constitute a signficant part of the series.

did.unittitle: Bugeaud, Patrick. "Wolman: Artiste séparatiste", master's thesis
Bugeaud, Patrick and Dominique Meens. "Wolman ou Les Inhumations ; genre moral"
"Gill Wolman: Inventeur de l'Art Scotch", in 3M brochure no. 28
Giroud, Michel. "Gil J. Wolman: entretien scotché Michel Giroud - Gil J. Wolman", printout with Wolman's corrections
Lepage, Jacques. "Anatomie de l'importance et l'influence de Raoul Hausmann"
"Les peintres parlents: Gil Wolman", in Les cahiers de la peinture no. 17
Prieto, Graciela. Excerpts from "Écritures du sinthome", PhD dissertation
Thieulin, Jérôme. "Étude du Fond Gil.J.Wolman au FRAC-Bourgogne: L'époque des «séparations» 1977-1980", master's thesis, Université de Bourgogne
Valensi, Michel. "Dix paroles après avoir lu Les Inhumations de Wolman"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies of the pamphlet published by Éditions Allia (Paris: 1995) and a photocopy of a thermofax typescript of Valensi text, accompanied by a thermofax typescript "Logique d'un promeneur" by Patrick Bugeaud on Dominique Meens' work L'ornitologie d'un promeneur .

did.unittitle: Wolman, Barbara. "Quelques avis sur la vidéo"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings and a text by Noël Arnaud introducing recordings by Wolman, Jean-Louis Brau, and François Dufrêne, found together in one file during processing.

did.unittitle: Essays submitted for Défense de mourir (Paris: Éditions Allia, 2001)
Botz, Yves. "Tu vas la taire ta gueule"
Semin, Didier. "C'est dans la pauvreté des choses qu'on fait la bonne soupe de l'oeuvre"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by correspondence between Emmanuel Latreille and Charlotte Wolman regarding the FRAC-Bourgogne's 2001 exhibit Gil Joseph Wolman: Les Ex-positions.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized alphabetically by document type. Or chronologically?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and empty envelopes kept by Wolman in a separate file as reference for addresses.

did.unittitle: Cadavres exquis and chess game notations
Continuing education cards at the Université de Paris
Death act and obituaries for Wolman
Death notices and obituary for Jean-Louis Brau and Louis Brau
Dedication page from Paul Éluard, Choix de poèmes (Paris: Gallimard, 1941)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With autograph dedication "À Joseph Wolman - en souvenirs, Paul Élouard". Accompanied by other pages from the volume.

did.unittitle: Dedication page from Jules Romains, autograph note to unknown recipient

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a membership card with the Union de la jeunesse juive (1944), cards for the Union française des auberges de la jeunesse (1947-1949), foreign currencies, bank receipts, transportation receipts, and undated holograph text on meeting Charlotte Wolman.

did.unittitle: Newsletter of the Comités des quartiers pour la paix au Vietnam
Newsletter of the Conseil local du mouvement de la paix (Aubervilliers)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly a cadavre exquis cut into its individual components.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains seventeen photographic prints of Wolman working in his studio, posing in front of his artwork, and posing with friends (Piero and Helena Simondo, Anne Zamire, Dominique Meens, Jean-Michel and Marie-Madeleine Mension).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one portrait of "Bernard Bengager dit Bubu", and one photocopy of a 1954 photograph of Pinot Gallizio.

did.unittitle: Share certificate ("Action artistique") for Stéphane Chaumet
Statutes and newsletter for the Société de la propriété artistique des dessins et modèles (SPADEM)
Wedding invitation for Wolman and Violette Brassard
